response: {"subfigures":[{"label":"b","description":"Bar chart showing the number of reads remaining after each sequential processing step in the eCLIP-seq pipeline, illustrating how PCR duplicate reads (same genomic position and same random-mer) are discarded to yield 'Usable reads'.","plot_type":"bar","plot_category":"categorical","x_axis":{"label":"Processing step","scale":"linear","units":null,"data_type":"categorical","is_inverted":false},"y_axis":{"label":"Number of reads","scale":"linear","units":null,"data_type":"continuous","is_inverted":false},"color_variable":null,"error_bars":"none","sample_size":null,"shows_individual_points":false,"data_source":null,"assays":["eCLIP-seq"],"supplementary_tables":[],"statistical_test":null,"facet_variable":null},{"label":"c","description":"Line/scatter plot showing the fraction of usable reads (after PCR duplicate removal) as a function of the number of uniquely mapped reads randomly sampled from RBFOX2 iCLIP and eCLIP experiments, with each point representing the mean of 100 downsampling experiments.","plot_type":"line","plot_category":"relational","x_axis":{"label":"Number of uniquely mapped reads sampled","scale":"linear","units":null,"data_type":"continuous","is_inverted":false},"y_axis":{"label":"Fraction of usable reads after PCR duplicate removal","scale":"linear","units":null,"data_type":"continuous","is_inverted":false},"color_variable":"Experiment type (iCLIP vs eCLIP)","error_bars":"sem","sample_size":"100 downsampling experiments per condition","shows_individual_points":true,"data_source":"RBFOX2 iCLIP and eCLIP experiments","assays":["eCLIP-seq","iCLIP"],"supplementary_tables":[],"statistical_test":null,"facet_variable":null}]}
